name: meridian
description: AI-powered git analytics, code hygiene, workflow engine, and chat assistant for VS Code

chatParticipants.name: meridian
chatParticipants.description: Git, hygiene, workflow, and agent assistant for your workspace

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show current git branch and file change counts

commands.name: scan
commands.description: Scan workspace for hygiene issues (large files, dead files, logs)

commands.name: agents
commands.description: List all available Meridian agents and their capabilities

commands.name: context
commands.description: Show workspace context: branch, active file, and available commands

commands.name: pr
commands.description: Generate a pull request description for the current branch

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review current branch changes — verdict and per-file comments

commands.name: briefing
commands.description: Generate a session briefing: branch state, recent commits, uncommitted work

commands.name: conflicts
commands.description: Suggest resolution strategies for detected git conflicts

commands.name: impact
commands.description: Trace the blast radius of a file or function change

commands.name: overview
commands.description: Session overview: branch state, recent commits, and session briefing

commands.name: prready
commands.description: PR readiness check: hygiene scan, code review, and PR description

commands.name: premerge
commands.description: Pre-merge check: inbound changes analysis and conflict resolution

commands.name: commit
commands.description: Smart-commit staged changes — grouped by semantic type

commands.name: inbound
commands.description: Analyze incoming remote changes for conflicts before merging

commands.name: cleanup
commands.description: Delete files flagged by the last hygiene scan (dry-run safe)

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns branch name, dirty/clean state, and counts of staged, unstaged, and untracked files. Good starting point to understand the current workspace state before other operations.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_smart_commit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Requires uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged) in the workspace. Analyzes diffs to group related changes and generates descriptive commit messages for each group. Interactive — presents groups for user approval before committing.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_analyze_inbound
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetches from the remote and analyzes what would change on merge. Returns counts of remote vs local changes, detected conflicts with severity, and recommendations. Call this before merging or pulling to understand risk. Pairs well with meridian_git_resolve_conflicts for conflict handling.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_hygiene_scan
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Scan the workspace for hygiene issues: dead/orphaned files not referenced by any import, large files exceeding size thresholds, log/temp files that may be stale, and dead code (unused exports, unreferenced functions). Returns categorized counts and highlighted examples.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_workflow_run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Execute a named workflow by running its steps sequentially through the command router. Returns per-step success/failure status, duration, and any errors. Use meridian_workflow_list first if you don't know the workflow name.

name.description: The exact name of the workflow to run

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_show_analytics
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate a comprehensive git analytics report covering commit frequency, code churn, author contributions, file volatility, and trend analysis. Opens an interactive webview panel with charts and tables. Optionally specify a time period.

period.description: Time period for the analytics report. Defaults to 3mo.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_chat_delegate
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Programmatic task router — classifies a natural language task description into a Meridian command and executes it. Intended for automated pipelines, workflows, and agent orchestration. For interactive use, prefer asking Copilot directly — it can invoke Meridian tools natively with better parameter handling.

task.description: Natural language description of what you want to do (e.g. 'generate a PR description', 'review my changes', 'session briefing')

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_generate_pr
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate a pull request description from the current branch's changes relative to the default branch. Analyzes commits, changed files, and diffs to produce a title, summary, change breakdown, and test plan in markdown. Returns structured PR content ready for review.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_review_pr
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Perform an AI code review of the current branch's changes. Returns a verdict (approve, request-changes, or comment) with per-file comments categorized by severity (critical, suggestion, nit). Use before opening a PR for self-review, or to review others' branches.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_comment_pr
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate granular inline code review comments for changed files in the current branch. More detailed than meridian_git_review_pr — produces file-level and line-level feedback. Use when you need specific, actionable comments rather than an overall verdict.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_resolve_conflicts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyze detected git merge conflicts and suggest resolution strategies per file. Returns keep-ours, keep-theirs, manual-merge, or review-needed with rationale for each. Call meridian_git_analyze_inbound first to detect conflicts, then use this tool for resolution guidance.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_session_briefing
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate a comprehensive morning session briefing covering current branch state, recent commit history, uncommitted changes, and any flags that need attention. Uses AI to produce a scannable markdown summary. Ideal as a start-of-day orientation.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_hygiene_impact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Trace the blast radius of changing a specific file or function using the TypeScript Compiler API. Returns importers, call sites, test file coverage, dependent file count, and a prose summary assessing risk. Essential before refactoring shared code.

filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the TypeScript file to analyze

functionName.description: Optional function or symbol name to trace call sites for

languageModelTools.name: meridian_agent_execute
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Execute a registered agent with a target command or workflow. The agent must have the target in its capabilities list. Use meridian_agent_list first to discover available agents and verify their capabilities before calling this tool.

agentId.description: The ID of the agent to execute

targetCommand.description: The command to execute (must be in agent's capabilities list)

targetWorkflow.description: The workflow to execute (alternative to targetCommand)

languageModelTools.name: meridian_workflow_list
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all available workflows defined in .vscode/workflows/ (workspace) and bundled with the extension. Returns names, descriptions, and step counts for each workflow. Call this first to discover available workflow names before using meridian_workflow_run.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_agent_list
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all registered agents defined in .vscode/agents/ (workspace) and bundled with the extension. Returns agent IDs, descriptions, and their declared capabilities (which commands they can execute). Call this first to discover available agents before using meridian_agent_execute.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_skill_overview
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a composite session overview that checks git status and generates a morning briefing in one call. Combines meridian_git_status and meridian_git_session_briefing into a single operation. Ideal as a start-of-day orientation.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_skill_pr_ready
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a composite PR readiness check that scans for hygiene issues, performs an AI code review, and generates a PR description — all in one call. Combines meridian_hygiene_scan, meridian_git_review_pr, and meridian_git_generate_pr. Use when preparing to open a pull request.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_skill_pre_merge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a composite pre-merge check that analyzes incoming remote changes and suggests conflict resolution strategies — all in one call. Combines meridian_git_analyze_inbound and meridian_git_resolve_conflicts. Use before merging or pulling to assess risk.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_export_json
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Export git analytics data as a JSON file. Includes commit frequency, author stats, file churn, and trend data. Optionally specify a time period.

period.description: Time period for the analytics report. Defaults to 3mo.

author.description: Optional: filter analytics by author name.

pathPattern.description: Optional: filter analytics by file path glob pattern.

languageModelTools.name: meridian_git_export_csv
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Export git analytics data as a CSV file. Includes commit frequency, author stats, file churn, and trend data. Optionally specify a time period.

period.description: Time period for the analytics report. Defaults to 3mo.

author.description: Optional: filter analytics by author name.

pathPattern.description: Optional: filter analytics by file path glob pattern.

meridian.model.default.description: Default language model family for all Meridian AI features (e.g. gpt-4o, claude-3-5-sonnet). Used when no domain-specific override is set.

meridian.model.hygiene.description: Language model family for hygiene domain (markdown review). Empty = inherits meridian.model.default.

meridian.model.git.description: Language model family for git domain (commit messages, diff analysis). Empty = inherits meridian.model.default.

meridian.model.chat.description: Language model family for @meridian chat participant direct LLM calls. Empty = inherits meridian.model.default.

meridian.security.lmEgress.mode.description: Controls LM egress behavior for Meridian AI features. allow = always send, prompt = require user confirmation, deny = block all LM sends.

meridian.security.gitNetwork.mode.description: Controls git network operations initiated by Meridian. allow = run automatically, prompt = require confirmation, deny = block fetch/pull.

meridian.security.gitNetwork.allowedHost.description: Optional allowlist of git remote hosts for Meridian network operations (e.g. github.com). Empty means no host restriction.

meridian.security.clipboard.autoCopy.description: Automatically copy generated output to clipboard when available.

meridian.security.logging.sensitive.description: Controls whether Meridian logs raw sensitive content or redacted output.

meridian.hygiene.prune.minAgeDays.description: Minimum file age in days for a file to be considered a prune candidate.

meridian.hygiene.prune.maxSizeMB.description: Files larger than this size (MB) are flagged as prune candidates when also older than minAgeDays.

meridian.hygiene.prune.minLineCount.description: Flag files with this many lines or more as prune candidates (when also older than minAgeDays). Set to 0 to disable.

meridian.hygiene.prune.categories.description: File categories that are automatically flagged as prune candidates when older than minAgeDays.

meridian.git.autofetch.description: Automatically fetch from remote before git operations.

meridian.git.branchClean.description: Automatically clean merged branches after pull.

meridian.hygiene.scanInterval.description: Interval in minutes between automatic hygiene scans.

meridian.chat.model.description: Default language model for chat operations.

meridian.chat.contextLines.description: Number of lines of context to include from the active file in chat requests.

meridian.sessionBriefing.autoLaunch.description: Automatically open a Session Briefing panel when the extension activates.

meridian.startup.enableFileWatchers.description: Enable startup registration of file watchers for automatic tree/status refresh.

meridian.startup.enableChatSurface.description: Enable startup registration of @meridian chat participant and LM tool surface.

meridian.log.level.description: Minimum log level for the Meridian output channel.

scripts.copy-assets: cp -r media out/media 2>/dev/null || true && cp -r bundled out/bundled 2>/dev/null || true && mkdir -p out/domains/git && rm -rf out/domains/git/analytics-ui && cp -r src/domains/git/analytics-ui out/domains/git/analytics-ui && cp node_modules/chart.js/dist/chart.umd.js out/domains/git/analytics-ui/chart.umd.js && rm -rf out/domains/git/session-briefing-ui && cp -r src/domains/git/session-briefing-ui out/domains/git/session-briefing-ui && mkdir -p out/domains/hygiene && rm -rf out/domains/hygiene/analytics-ui && cp -r src/domains/hygiene/analytics-ui out/domains/hygiene/analytics-ui && cp node_modules/chart.js/dist/chart.umd.js out/domains/hygiene/analytics-ui/chart.umd.js
